senses.definition: used to discuss the relationship or rights of parties involved in a legal matter or contract

examples.x: As between the landlord and tenant, the lease agreement clearly outlines the responsibilities of each party.
The agreement specifies the division of assets as between the spouses in case of divorce.
The court will determine the liabilities as between the partners in the business dispute.
The contract addresses the allocation of risks as between the parties involved.
The partnership agreement outlines the duties and obligations as between the partners.

senses.definition: used to indicate accordance with a particular standard, rule, or instruction

examples.x: As per company policy, all employees must attend the mandatory training session.
The project must be completed as per the client's specifications.
All employees are expected to dress professionally as per the company dress code.
The event will begin at 9:00 AM as per the schedule.
Please submit your report as per the formatting guidelines provided.

senses.definition: causing a negative consequence or cost to someone or something in order to benefit another

examples.x: His success came at the expense of his personal life, as he devoted all his time to work.
She pursued her career goals relentlessly, often at the expense of her personal relationships.
The company prioritized profit margins at the expense of employee satisfaction.
His success came at the expense of his health, as he sacrificed sleep and exercise to meet deadlines.
The politician's popularity rose, but it came at the expense of his integrity.

senses.definition: in agreement with a particular standard, guideline, or objective

examples.x: Our actions are in alignment with our company's mission statement, which emphasizes sustainability and social responsibility.
The new policy is in alignment with industry regulations.
The project plan is in alignment with the client's requirements.
Our goals are in alignment with the overall strategy of the organization.
The curriculum is designed in alignment with educational standards.

senses.definition: in partnership with a particular person, organization, or entity

examples.x: The event was organized in association with several local businesses.
The research project is conducted in association with several universities.
The charity fundraiser is held in association with various corporate sponsors.
The concert is presented in association with the city's symphony orchestra.
The workshop is hosted in association with the industry-leading experts.

senses.definition: in harmony with a particular idea, principle, or concept

examples.x: The new policy is in coherence with our company's commitment to environmental sustainability.
Our actions are in coherence with our values and beliefs.
The proposed changes are in coherence with the overall strategy of the organization.
The design of the building is in coherence with the surrounding environment.
His remarks were in coherence with the theme of the conference.
The medication should be taken in combination with a healthy diet for the best results.
Exercise, in combination with a balanced lifestyle, promotes overall well-being.
The new product is effective when used in combination with other skincare products.
The therapy is most effective when used in combination with counseling.
The software offers advanced features when used in combination with specific hardware.

senses.definition: used to covey that two or more people or things are working together to achieve a common goal

examples.x: The two departments worked in concert with each other to streamline the project's workflow.
The team members are coordinating their efforts in concert with the project manager to meet the deadline.
The countries are collaborating in concert with international organizations to address climate change.
The stakeholders are working in concert with the developers to launch the new product.
The community is coming together in concert with local authorities to combat crime.

senses.definition: in agreement with something else, indicating that two or more things are happening simultaneously

examples.x: The new policies were implemented in concurrence with the recommendations of the advisory board.
Her actions were in concurrence with her words, demonstrating sincerity.
The decision was made in concurrence with the recommendations of the advisory board.
The new regulations are in concurrence with international standards for safety.
The decisions made by the committee are in concurrence with the bylaws of the organization.

senses.definition: in combination or partnership with another

examples.x: The marketing team is working in conjunction with the design department to create a new advertising campaign.
The research study was conducted in conjunction with several universities.
The medication is prescribed in conjunction with lifestyle changes for optimal results.
The project will be completed in conjunction with our strategic partners.
The course materials are used in conjunction with online tutorials for comprehensive learning.

senses.definition: used to indicate a relationship or association between two or more things

examples.x: The investigation is ongoing in connection with the recent data breach.
The new evidence was found in connection with the ongoing investigation.
The decision was made in connection with the company's long-term goals.
He mentioned the incident in connection with his concerns about safety.
The announcement was made in connection with the company's quarterly earnings report.

senses.definition: in alignment with a particular idea, principle, or concept

examples.x: The company's values are in harmony with its commitment to social responsibility.
Her actions are in harmony with her values and beliefs.
The new policy is in harmony with the company's mission statement.
The design of the building is in harmony with the surrounding environment.
His remarks were in harmony with the theme of the conference.

senses.definition: in accordance with a particular style, tradition, or expectation

examples.x: The restaurant's menu is in keeping with its reputation for offering innovative cuisine.
The party decorations were in keeping with the theme of the event.
His attire was in keeping with the formal dress code of the gala.
The music played at the wedding ceremony was in keeping with the couple's cultural heritage.

senses.definition: with a similar attitude, mindset, or intention as a particular concept or principle

examples.x: In the spirit of the holidays, the community organized a charity drive to help those in need.
The decision was made in the spirit of fairness and equity.
In the spirit of celebration, the city hosted a parade to honor its founding anniversary.
She donated money to the charity in the spirit of generosity.
In the spirit of tradition, the family gathered for a holiday feast to honor their cultural heritage.

senses.definition: following a specific rule, command, or authority

examples.x: The soldiers acted in obedience to their commanding officer's orders during the mission.
She completed the task in obedience to the instructions given by her supervisor.
The students remained quiet in obedience to the teacher's request.
He signed the contract in obedience to the terms and conditions outlined.
The employees followed safety protocols in obedience to company policies.

senses.definition: in addition to or along with

examples.x: She went to the concert together with her friends.
The new policy, together with the existing regulations, will ensure better compliance.
He brought his laptop together with a set of important documents.
The teacher, together with the students, organized a charity event.
The recipe calls for flour, together with sugar and eggs, to make the cake.
